evals.prompt: We just landed a ledger-cli read-path change this session: `append-journal <id> <text>` is renamed to `journal add <id> <text>`, and `show task <id>` no longer includes the `<info added on …>` journal blocks by default — you now pass `--journal` to get them. Propagate this across the dev-lifecycle skills and agents so next session's agents aren't reading the old commands.
evals.expected_output: A confirmed change summary, a grep-driven sweep of the canonical .claude/skills + .claude/agents and docs-site .claude/skills surface, minimal voice-preserving edits swapping the stale tokens, a per-file report (patched / flagged / discarded), and a post-edit re-grep showing zero unjustified survivors of the old tokens.

evals.prompt: The server-ledger cutover (id-90) is going in — `bun scripts/ledger-cli.ts` is being replaced by the task-view patch-server. Which skills and agents reference ledger-cli today, and what would a propagation of this cutover have to touch? Don't edit anything yet, just map it.
evals.expected_output: A discovery-only pass: reads references/surface-map.md, re-greps the workflow surface for ledger-cli references, and returns the per-file list of dependents (both repos) with the command verbs each uses, distinguishing true ledger references from homographs, and noting this is a map-only request (no patches).
